title: In a Trump-Endorsed Presidential Candidate, Colombia’s Women See a Familiar Far-Right Playbook
description: Colombia’s presidential front-runner has sparked a debate about masculinity and machismo. Women’s rights groups see a familiar right-wing playbook.
author: Annie Correal

title: Sex, Lies and Secrets: A Federal Judge’s Trysts Go Public
description: Now, Judge Eleanor Ross’s career and caseload are under scrutiny. And her punishment, a private reprimand, has sparked backlash.
author: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs and Mattathias Schwartz

title: A Youthful Crop of Politicians Run on Housing, and Their Own Rent
description: A cohort of young Millennial and Gen Z politicians have centered their campaigns this year on housing costs, and the divide is more generational than partisan.
author: Kate Zernike

title: Conor McGregor’s Comeback: A Tale of Banned Drugs and a Famous Doctor
description: McGregor, the Ultimate Fighting Championship’s main attraction, had the support of the prominent sports physician Neal ElAttrache when he decided to use performance-enhancing drugs.
author: Michael S. Schmidt

title: Federal Judge Blocks Alabama From Using Nitrogen Gas in Jeffrey Lee Execution
description: The judge ordered the state to find an alternate method if it is going to move ahead with the execution of Jeffery Lee, who was convicted of murder, and an appeals court upheld the decision.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Pro-Palestinian Activists at U. of Michigan Indicted on Federal Conspiracy Charges
description: The new charges appeared to signify an escalation in the federal government’s approach to campus activism.
author: Mitch Smith

title: No, There Was Not a Snake Invasion at the Swiss Team’s World Cup Training Facility
description: A map of the San Diego facility that the team is using as its base led people to believe that snakes had infiltrated the premises.
author: Madaleine Rubin

title: Top Pentagon Official Worked Closely With C.I.A. Officer Later Found With Gold Bars
description: David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, worked on a highly classified China spying program with Stephen A. Feinberg, the Pentagon’s second-ranking official.
author: Mark Mazzetti and Julian E. Barnes

title: Burning Cross Is Found in Downtown Chicago Park
description: No one was injured by the burning cross, which was found in Grant Park, the authorities said. The police and the F.B.I. are investigating.
author: John S.W. MacDonald and Mark Walker

title: Democrats Try to Move Past ‘Cultural Pandering’ to Latinos for Midterm Elections
description: The party is trying desperately to win back some voters in the key swing group.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: Suspect in the Killing of a Minnesota Lawmaker Strikes a Plea Deal
description: The arrangement means that Vance Boelter will not face the death penalty after being charged with killing Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark.
author: Mitch Smith

title: FISA Surveillance Law May Expire After Trump Picks Bill Pulte for Intel Post
description: Republicans are struggling to extend a powerful surveillance authority set to lapse this weekend after President Trump alienated lawmakers with his choice of acting spy chief.
author: Dustin Volz and Robert Jimison

title: Maine Senate Race Heats Up as Trump Attacks Platner
description: With Graham Platner now the Democratic nominee against Senator Susan Collins, signs of how hostile the race will be have quickly emerged.
author: Lisa Lerer and Bayliss Wagner

title: Hegseth Visits Guantánamo Bay Amid U.S. Tensions With Cuba
description: The trip comes as the Trump administration pushes for political and economic changes on the island.
author: Carol Rosenberg

title: Will Trump’s Repairs to Washington D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Work?
description: President Trump has said that construction is complete. Now comes the real test. Will the longstanding problems finally be solved?
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: F.B.I. Searches California Plant Where Chemical Tank Nearly Exploded
description: Federal agents were looking for records that might shed light on why chemicals overheated, threatening a catastrophe in Orange County.
author: Tim Arango and Livia Albeck-Ripka

title: Southern Baptists Move to Strengthen Ban on Women Pastors
description: The amendment passed easily at the denomination’s annual gathering. It needs approval again next year to enter the constitution.
author: Ruth Graham

title: University of Florida Chooses Stuart Bell for President Over Some Conservative Criticism
description: The nominee, Stuart Bell, led the University of Alabama for a decade, where he embraced diversity initiatives but also Donald J. Trump.
author: Stephanie Saul and Alan Blinder

title: Mace Defeat Heralds an Exodus of Rabble-Rousers From Congress
description: The Republican congresswoman from South Carolina, who made enemies on both sides of the political aisle, is just one of several flashy lawmakers to be leaving Capitol Hill.
author: Annie Karni

title: He has one goooooal.
author: The New York Times
